article.title: Mistral Renames Le Chat to Vibe, Folding Chat, Office Automation, and Cloud Coding Into One Agent
article.summary: Mistral rebranded its Le Chat assistant as Vibe, a single agent spanning Work Mode for office tasks and Code Mode for remote coding.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Mistral AI has renamed its Le Chat assistant as Vibe, repositioning what had been a conversational chatbot into a single agent that handles office work and software development. According to [The Decoder](https://the-decoder.com/mistral-rebrands-lechat-as-vibe-betting-its-chatbots-future-is-as-a-full-blown-work-agent/), Mistral is "renaming Le Chat to Vibe, repositioning the former chatbot as a work tool that handles emails, writes reports, and ships code all the way to the pull request stage." As reported by [WinBuzzer](https://winbuzzer.com/2026/06/01/mistral-rebrands-le-chat-as-vibe-for-work-and-coding-xcxwbn/), the French lab "has renamed its Le Chat AI assistant into Vibe and unified its offering into an AI agent" — a move WinBuzzer characterizes as "a product-packaging test rather than a simple brand swap."

## What We Know

Vibe is split into two modes. In its work-focused mode, the agent connects to common enterprise tools. According to [The Decoder](https://the-decoder.com/mistral-rebrands-lechat-as-vibe-betting-its-chatbots-future-is-as-a-full-blown-work-agent/), Vibe "hooks into Google Workspace, Outlook, SharePoint, Slack, or GitHub and runs tasks from there, scanning inboxes, pulling numbers from a spreadsheet, building a report, and pushing it to Notion or SharePoint." [WinBuzzer](https://winbuzzer.com/2026/06/01/mistral-rebrands-le-chat-as-vibe-for-work-and-coding-xcxwbn/) reports that the work mode integrates "Google Workspace and GitHub connectors alongside Outlook, SharePoint, Slack, and custom connectors," and that users can "review a task plan before execution, inspect each step, schedule recurring jobs" before the agent runs.

The second mode targets software development. [The Decoder](https://the-decoder.com/mistral-rebrands-lechat-as-vibe-betting-its-chatbots-future-is-as-a-full-blown-work-agent/) reports that the coding agents "build features, fix bugs, write tests, and open a pull request when they're done." According to [WinBuzzer](https://winbuzzer.com/2026/06/01/mistral-rebrands-le-chat-as-vibe-for-work-and-coding-xcxwbn/), the coding mode "runs remote coding sessions in isolated sandboxes" that persist in the cloud, and "sessions that start from Slack are slated for June 2026."

Under the hood, the agent runs on Mistral's flagship model. [The Decoder](https://the-decoder.com/mistral-rebrands-lechat-as-vibe-betting-its-chatbots-future-is-as-a-full-blown-work-agent/) notes that "Mistral Medium 3.5 can toggle between a fast mode and a more thorough one through `reasoning_effort`." The Machine Herald [previously reported](/article/2026-05/06-mistral-medium-35-folds-chat-reasoning-and-coding-into-a-single-128-billion-parameter-open-weight-flagship) on Mistral Medium 3.5 when the company released it as a single open-weight flagship combining chat, reasoning, and coding.

On pricing, [The Decoder](https://the-decoder.com/mistral-rebrands-lechat-as-vibe-betting-its-chatbots-future-is-as-a-full-blown-work-agent/) reports a tiered structure in which "Pro runs 14.99 euros a month, Team is 24.99 euros per user per month, and Enterprise" is priced on request, alongside a free tier. [WinBuzzer](https://winbuzzer.com/2026/06/01/mistral-rebrands-le-chat-as-vibe-for-work-and-coding-xcxwbn/) adds that a "student plan priced at 5.99 per month" offers a lower entry point.

## What We Don't Know

Neither source details Vibe's usage limits across the free and paid tiers, leaving the practical ceiling on agent runs unclear. The timing of broader feature availability is also only partly pinned down: WinBuzzer notes Slack-initiated coding sessions are scheduled for June 2026, but the rollout cadence for other capabilities is not fully specified.

## Analysis

The rebrand reframes Mistral's consumer-facing chatbot as an agent platform aimed at the enterprise market. According to [The Decoder](https://the-decoder.com/mistral-rebrands-lechat-as-vibe-betting-its-chatbots-future-is-as-a-full-blown-work-agent/), bundling chat, work automation, and coding "under one brand" puts "Mistral in more direct competition with the agentic versions of ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude." [WinBuzzer](https://winbuzzer.com/2026/06/01/mistral-rebrands-le-chat-as-vibe-for-work-and-coding-xcxwbn/) frames the test ahead as whether "one package can handle office automation and remote coding without making either side feel secondary."
